     The Numbers Business Report

My Week with Marilyn (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $14,597,405Details
International Box Office $19,643,167
Worldwide Box Office $34,240,572
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,519,631
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,547,200
Total $8,066,831
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the early summer of 1956, 23 year-old Colin Clark, just down from Oxford and determined to make his way in the film business, worked as a lowly assistant on the set of 'The Prince and the Showgirl'. The film that famously united Sir Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe, who was also on honeymoon with her new husband, the playwright Aurthur Miller. Nearly 40 years on, his diary account The Prince, the Showgirl and Me was published, but one week was missing and this was published some years later as My Week with Marilyn - this is the story of that week. When Arthur Miller leaves England, the coast is clear for Colin to introduce Marilyn to some of the pleasures of British life; an idyllic week in which he escorted a Monroe desperate to get away from her retinue of Hollywood hangers-on and the pressures of work.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,750,507 (12.0% of total gross)
Legs:8.34 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:42.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$10,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:244 opening theaters/630 max. theaters, 7.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $21,983,580

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 23rd, 2011 (Limited) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: February 24th, 2012 (Wide) (Spain)
March 7th, 2012 (Wide) (Belgium)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (New Zealand)
April 19th, 2012 (Wide) (Germany)
April 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Austria)
Video Release: March 13th, 2012 by Anchor Bay Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for mature thematic material.
(Rating bulletin 2193, 10/12/2011)
Running Time: 99 minutes
Plot point: Biography, Performing Arts, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance, Troubled Pregnancy or Miscarriage
Social setting: Movie Business
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Weinstein Company, BBC Films, Trademark Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

In the early summer of 1956, 23 year-old Colin Clark, just down from Oxford and determined to make his way in the film business, worked as a lowly assistant on the set of 'The Prince and the Showgirl'. The film that famously united Sir Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe, who was also on honeymoon with her new husband, the playwright Aurthur Miller. Nearly 40 years on, his diary account The Prince, the Showgirl and Me was published, but one week was missing and this was published some years later as My Week with Marilyn - this is the story of that week. When Arthur Miller leaves England, the coast is clear for Colin to introduce Marilyn to some of the pleasures of British life; an idyllic week in which he escorted a Monroe desperate to get away from her retinue of Hollywood hangers-on and the pressures of work.
